title: satureja vulgaris proles glandulosa
intro: Satureja vulgaris proles glandulosa, also known as mountain savory, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its small, grayish-green leaves and tiny white or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of mountain savory include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of mountain savory include relieving respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as digestive issues, like bloating and indigestion, due to its carminative and expectorant properties. Mountain savory can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
